ActivateCertificateAuthorityRequest.description: Request payload for activating a Certificate Authority with a signed certificate.

certificateChain.description: Certificate chain in PEM format for Bring Your Own activation, including the signed certificate. The first certificate must be the signed certificate matching the CSR generated by the service, followed by any intermediate CAs, and optionally the root CA. Certificates must be ordered from leaf to root and concatenated in PEM format.

properties.description: The resource-specific properties for this resource.
The resource-specific properties for this resource.

AssetEndpointProfileListResult.description: The response of a AssetEndpointProfile list operation.

value.description: The AssetEndpointProfile items on this page

nextLink.description: The link to the next page of items

AssetEndpointProfileProperties.description: Defines the Asset Endpoint Profile properties.

targetAddress.description: The local valid URI specifying the network address/DNS name of a southbound device. The scheme part of the targetAddress URI specifies the type of the device. The additionalConfiguration field holds further connector type specific configuration.

endpointProfileType.description: Defines the configuration for the connector type that is being used with the endpoint profile.

authentication.description: Defines the client authentication mechanism to the server.

additionalConfiguration.description: Stringified JSON that contains connectivity type specific further configuration (e.g., OPC UA, ONVIF).

discoveredAssetEndpointProfileRef.description: Reference to a discovered asset endpoint profile. Populated only if the asset endpoint profile has been created from discovery flow. Discovered asset endpoint profile name must be provided.

status.description: Read only object to reflect changes that have occurred on the Edge. Similar to Kubernetes status property for custom resources.

AssetEndpointProfileStatus.description: Defines the asset endpoint profile status properties.

errors.description: Array object to transfer and persist errors that originate from the Edge.

AssetEndpointProfileStatusError.description: Defines the asset endpoint profile status error properties.

code.description: Error code for classification of errors (ex: 400, 404, 500, etc.).

message.description: Human readable helpful error message to provide additional context for error (ex: “targetAddress 'foo' is not a valid url”).

AssetEndpointProfileUpdate.description: The type used for update operations of the AssetEndpointProfile.

properties.description: The resource-specific properties for this resource.

AssetEndpointProfileUpdateProperties.description: The updatable properties of the AssetEndpointProfile.

targetAddress.description: The local valid URI specifying the network address/DNS name of a southbound device. The scheme part of the targetAddress URI specifies the type of the device. The additionalConfiguration field holds further connector type specific configuration.

endpointProfileType.description: Defines the configuration for the connector type that is being used with the endpoint profile.

additionalConfiguration.description: Stringified JSON that contains connectivity type specific further configuration (e.g., OPC UA, ONVIF).

authentication.description: Object that describes the default user authentication information for the asset endpoint profile.

method.description: Defines the method to authenticate the user of the client at the server.

usernamePasswordCredentials.description: Defines the username and password references when UsernamePassword user authentication mode is selected.

x509Credentials.description: Defines the certificate reference when Certificate user authentication mode is selected.

AssetListResult.description: The response of a Asset list operation.

value.description: The Asset items on this page

nextLink.description: The link to the next page of items

externalAssetId.description: Asset id provided by the customer.

assetEndpointProfileRef.description: A reference to the asset endpoint profile (connection information) used by brokers to connect to an endpoint that provides data points for this asset. Must provide asset endpoint profile name.

version.description: An integer that is incremented each time the resource is modified.

attributes.description: A set of key-value pairs that contain custom attributes set by the customer.

discoveredAssetRefs.description: Reference to a list of discovered assets. Populated only if the asset has been created from discovery flow. Discovered asset names must be provided.

defaultDatasetsConfiguration.description: Stringified JSON that contains connector-specific default configuration for all datasets. Each dataset can have its own configuration that overrides the default settings here.

defaultEventsConfiguration.description: Stringified JSON that contains connector-specific default configuration for all events. Each event can have its own configuration that overrides the default settings here.

defaultTopic.description: Object that describes the default topic information for the asset.

datasets.description: Array of datasets that are part of the asset. Each dataset describes the data points that make up the set.

events.description: Array of events that are part of the asset. Each event can have per-event configuration.

status.description: Read only object to reflect changes that have occurred on the Edge. Similar to Kubernetes status property for custom resources.

errors.description: Array object to transfer and persist errors that originate from the Edge.

version.description: A read only incremental counter indicating the number of times the configuration has been modified from the perspective of the current actual (Edge) state of the Asset. Edge would be the only writer of this value and would sync back up to the cloud. In steady state, this should equal version.

datasets.description: Array of dataset statuses that describe the status of each dataset.

events.description: Array of event statuses that describe the status of each event.

AssetStatusDataset.description: Defines the asset status dataset properties.

name.description: The name of the dataset. Must be unique within the status.datasets array. This name is used to correlate between the spec and status dataset information.

AssetStatusError.description: Defines the asset status error properties.

code.description: Error code for classification of errors (ex: 400, 404, 500, etc.).

message.description: Human readable helpful error message to provide additional context for error (ex: “capability Id 'foo' does not exist”).

AssetStatusEvent.description: Defines the asset status event properties.

name.description: The name of the event. Must be unique within the status.events array. This name is used to correlate between the spec and status event information.

AssetUpdate.description: The type used for update operations of the Asset.

properties.description: The resource-specific properties for this resource.

AssetUpdateProperties.description: The updatable properties of the Asset.

attributes.description: A set of key-value pairs that contain custom attributes set by the customer.

defaultDatasetsConfiguration.description: Stringified JSON that contains connector-specific default configuration for all datasets. Each dataset can have its own configuration that overrides the default settings here.

defaultEventsConfiguration.description: Stringified JSON that contains connector-specific default configuration for all events. Each event can have its own configuration that overrides the default settings here.

datasets.description: Array of datasets that are part of the asset. Each dataset describes the data points that make up the set.

events.description: Array of events that are part of the asset. Each event can have per-event configuration.

defaultTopic.description: Object that describes the default topic information for the asset.

path.description: The topic path for messages published to an MQTT broker.

retain.description: When set to 'Keep', messages published to an MQTT broker will have the retain flag set. Default: 'Never'.

Authentication.description: Definition of the client authentication mechanism to the server.

method.description: Defines the method to authenticate the user of the client at the server.

values.name: Anonymous
values.description: The user authentication method is anonymous.

values.name: Certificate
values.description: The user authentication method is an x509 certificate.

values.name: UsernamePassword
values.description: The user authentication method is a username and password.

usernamePasswordCredentials.description: Defines the username and password references when UsernamePassword user authentication mode is selected.

x509Credentials.description: Defines the certificate reference when Certificate user authentication mode is selected.

AuthenticationMethod.description: The method to authenticate the user of the client at the server.

values.name: Anonymous
values.description: The user authentication method is anonymous.

values.name: Certificate
values.description: The user authentication method is an x509 certificate.

values.name: UsernamePassword
values.description: The user authentication method is a username and password.

BringYourOwnCertificateAuthorityProperti.description: Properties for a Certificate Authority whose CA certificate is signed by an external issuer.

BringYourOwnConfiguration.description: Configuration for Bring Your Own Certificate Authority. When enabled, the Certificate Authority certificate is signed by an external issuer.

certificateSigningRequest.description: Certificate Signing Request (CSR) in PEM format, generated by the service.

thumbprint.description: Thumbprint of the Certificate Authority certificate signed by the external issuer.

status.description: The status of the Bring Your Own certificate lifecycle.

BrokerStateStoreDestinationConfiguration.description: The configuration for a MQTT broker state store destination.

key.description: The MQTT broker state store destination key.

validityNotBefore.description: Certificate is valid not before this date. Format ISO8601. Generated based on on validity period.

validityNotAfter.description: Certificate is valid not after this date. Format ISO8601. Generated based on validity period.

provisioningState.description: The status of the last operation.

uuid.description: Globally unique, immutable, non-reusable identifier of the Certificate Authority resource.

certificateAuthorityType.description: The Certificate Authority type. Use Root for a self-signed, service-managed root CA, ICA for a CA signed by another same-namespace Certificate Authority, and BringYourOwn for a CA certificate signed by an external issuer.

validityNotBefore.description: Certificate is valid not before this date. Format ISO8601.

validityNotAfter.description: Certificate is valid not after this date. Format ISO8601.

CertificateAuthorityStatus.description: Status of the Certificate Authority certificate lifecycle.

values.name: PendingActivation
values.description: The CSR has been generated and is waiting for activation.

values.name: ActiveButPendingRenewal
values.description: The Certificate Authority is active but requires renewal.

values.name: ICA
values.description: Intermediate Certificate Authority signed by another Certificate Authority.

values.name: BringYourOwn
values.description: Intermediate Certificate Authority signed by an external issuer.

observabilityMode.description: An indication of how the data point should be mapped to OpenTelemetry.

name.description: The name of the data point.

dataSource.description: The address of the source of the data in the asset (e.g. URL) so that a client can access the data source on the asset.

dataPointConfiguration.description: Stringified JSON that contains connector-specific configuration for the data point. For OPC UA, this could include configuration like, publishingInterval, samplingInterval, and queueSize.

DataPointObservabilityMode.description: Defines the data point observability mode.

name.description: Name of the dataset.

datasetConfiguration.description: Stringified JSON that contains connector-specific JSON string that describes configuration for the specific dataset.

topic.description: Object that describes the topic information for the specific dataset.

dataPoints.description: Array of data points that are part of the dataset. Each data point can have per-data point configuration.

DatasetBrokerStateStoreDestination.description: The type for a MQTT broker state store destination.

configuration.description: The MQTT broker state store destination configuration.

DatasetDestinationTarget.description: The set of supported dataset destinations for an asset.

DatasetMqttDestination.description: The type for a MQTT destination.

DatasetStorageDestination.description: The type for a storage destination.

uuid.description: A unique identifier for the device.

enabled.description: Indicates if the resource is enabled or not.

externalDeviceId.description: The Device ID provided by the customer.

attributes.description: A set of key-value pairs that contain custom attributes set by the customer.

DeviceCredentialPolicy.description: Defines the Policy used to issue device certificates if any.

endpointType.description: Type of connection used for the messaging endpoint.

address.description: The endpoint address to connect to.

externalDeviceId.description: The Device ID provided by the customer. Also used by IoT Hub to hold the IoT Hub device ID.

softwareRevision.description: The software revision for this device.

DeviceRef.description: Defines which device and endpoint to use for this asset

endpointName.description: The name of endpoint to use

DeviceRefUpdate.description: Defines which device and endpoint to use for this asset

endpointName.description: The name of endpoint to use

config.description: Defines the device status config properties.

endpoints.description: Defines the device status for inbound/outbound endpoints.

error.description: Defines the error related to this endpoint.

DeviceStatusEndpoints.description: Defines the device status for inbound/outbound endpoints.

inbound.description: KeyValue pair representing status of inbound endpoints.

DiscoveredInboundEndpoints.description: An endpoint to connect to the device.

address.description: The endpoint address & port. This can be either an IP address (e.g., 192.168.1.1) or a fully qualified domain name (FQDN, e.g., server.example.com).

version.description: Protocol version associated with the endpoint e.g. 1 or 2 for endpointType Microsoft.HTTP, and 3.5 or 5.0 for endpointType Microsoft.Mqtt etc.

supportedAuthenticationMethods.description: List of supported authentication methods supported by device for Inbound connections.

additionalConfiguration.description: Stringified JSON that contains configuration to be used by the connector (e.g., OPC UA, ONVIF).

lastUpdatedOn.description: The timestamp (in UTC) when the endpoint was discovered.

DiscoveredInboundEndpointsUpdate.description: An endpoint to connect to the device.

address.description: The endpoint address & port. This can be either an IP address (e.g., 192.168.1.1) or a fully qualified domain name (FQDN, e.g., server.example.com).

version.description: Protocol version associated with the endpoint e.g. 1 or 2 for endpointType Microsoft.HTTP, and 3.5 or 5.0 for endpointType Microsoft.Mqtt etc.

supportedAuthenticationMethods.description: List of supported authentication methods supported by device for Inbound connections.

additionalConfiguration.description: Stringified JSON that contains configuration to be used by the connector (e.g., OPC UA, ONVIF).

lastUpdatedOn.description: The timestamp (in UTC) when the endpoint was discovered.

DiscoveredMessagingEndpoints.description: Connection endpoint URL a device can use to connect to a service.

inbound.description: Set of endpoints to connect to the device.

outbound.description: Set of endpoints a device can connect to.

Error.description: Defines the error object with properties.

code.description: Error code for classification of errors (ex: '400', '404', '500', etc.).

message.description: Human-readable helpful error message to provide additional context for error (e.g.,: “Capability ID 'foo' does not exist”).

details.description: Array of error details that describe the status of each error.

code.description: Multi-part error code for classification and root causing of errors (ex: 400.200.100.432).

message.description: Human-readable helpful error message to provide additional context for error (ex: “Authentication method not supported”).

info.description: Human-readable helpful detailed text context for debugging (ex: “The following mechanisms are supported...”).

correlationId.description: Unique identifier for the transaction to aid in debugging.

observabilityMode.description: An indication of how the event should be mapped to OpenTelemetry.

name.description: The name of the event.

eventNotifier.description: The address of the notifier of the event in the asset (e.g. URL) so that a client can access the event on the asset.

eventConfiguration.description: Stringified JSON that contains connector-specific configuration for the event. For OPC UA, this could include configuration like, publishingInterval, samplingInterval, and queueSize.

topic.description: Object that describes the topic information for the specific event.

EventDestinationTarget.description: The set of supported event destinations for an asset.

EventMqttDestination.description: The type for a MQTT destination.

EventStorageDestination.description: The type for a storage destination.

name.description: The extended location name.

values.name: RefreshingMembers
values.description: The group is refreshing its members.

GroupPreviewMembersResponse.description: Response for the preview members operation.

members.description: A preview of member resource IDs.

displayName.description: Human-readable display name for the group.

groupType.description: The type of group that determines what kind of resources can be members.

lastMembershipRefreshTime.description: A timestamp indicating the last time the group membership was refreshed.

membershipState.description: The membership state of the group.

query.description: The query string used to determine group membership.

displayName.description: Human-readable display name for the group.

HealthState.description: Represents the health state of a resource.

status.description: The high-level health status of the resource.

lastTransitionTime.description: The timestamp (RFC3339) when the health status last changed.

lastUpdateTime.description: The timestamp (RFC3339) when the health status was last updated, even if the status did not change.

message.description: A human-readable message describing the last transition.

reasonCode.description: Unique, CamelCase reason code describing the cause of the last health state transition.

HealthStatus.description: Defines the health state of the resource.

values.name: Available
values.description: Resource is Available and functioning as expected.

values.name: Unavailable
values.description: Resource is not functioning as expected.

HostAuthentication.description: Definition of the client authentication mechanism to the host.

method.description: Defines the method to authenticate the user of the client at the server.

values.name: Anonymous
values.description: The user authentication method is anonymous.

values.name: Certificate
values.description: The user authentication method is an x509 certificate.

values.name: UsernamePassword
values.description: The user authentication method is a username and password.

usernamePasswordCredentials.description: Defines the username and password references when UsernamePassword user authentication mode is selected.

x509Credentials.description: Defines the certificate reference when Certificate user authentication mode is selected.

InboundCallerIdentity.description: The identity a linked resource uses for inbound calls to the ADR namespace.

userAssignedIdentity.description: The resource ID of the user-assigned managed identity. Required when type is UserAssigned.

InboundCallerIdentityType.description: The type of identity used for inbound calls to the ADR namespace.

InboundEndpoints.description: An endpoint to connect to the device.

address.description: The endpoint address & port. This can be either an IP address (e.g., 192.168.1.1) or a fully qualified domain name (FQDN, e.g., server.example.com).

version.description: Protocol version associated with the endpoint e.g. 1 or 2 for endpointType Microsoft.HTTP, and 3.5 or 5.0 for endpointType Microsoft.Mqtt etc.

authentication.description: Defines the client authentication mechanism to the server.

trustSettings.description: Defines server trust settings for the endpoint.

additionalConfiguration.description: Stringified JSON that contains configuration to be used by the connector (e.g., OPC UA, ONVIF).

trustSettings.description: Defines server trust settings for the endpoint.

additionalConfiguration.description: Stringified JSON that contains configuration to be used by the connector (e.g., OPC UA, ONVIF).

JobListResult.description: The response of a Job list operation.

value.description: The Job items on this page

nextLink.description: The link to the next page of items

jobType.description: The type of the job. Set at create time; immutable thereafter.

properties.description: The resource-specific properties for this resource.

JobRunCancellationReason.description: The reason a job run was canceled. Present only when status is Canceled.

values.name: CanceledByCustomer
values.description: The customer canceled the run via the cancel action.

values.name: CanceledByAnotherJob
values.description: The run was canceled because another job superseded it. For an Update job this happens when another Update job targeting the same group is scheduled; for a RecoveryUpdate job this happens when another RecoveryUpdate job for the same compatible devices is scheduled.

values.name: CanceledByGroupDelete
values.description: The run was canceled because the target group was deleted. This reason does not apply to RecoveryUpdate jobs, which do not target a group.

JobRunListResult.description: The response of a JobRun list operation.

value.description: The JobRun items on this page

nextLink.description: The link to the next page of items

status.description: The status of the job run.

cancellationReason.description: The reason the run was canceled. Present only when status is Canceled.

scheduledTime.description: The scheduled time for the job run.

startTime.description: The start time of the job run.

endTime.description: The end time of the job run.

results.description: The results summary of the job run.

JobRunResultItem.description: A single device result entry from a job run.

deviceUuid.description: The device UUID. ADR returns the device's stable UUID identifier (ADU surfaces device UUIDs in its private API; ADR re-emits them under this name to align with ADR-wide identifier conventions).

status.description: The current per-device status for this run. One of: Succeeded, Failed, InProgress, Canceled, NotApplied.

reason.description: Short, machine-readable diagnostic that pairs with status. Present only when status is Failed; an ADU-provided machine-readable code (e.g. DOWNLOAD_FAILED). Omitted for all other statuses, including Succeeded and InProgress.

JobRunResultListResponse.description: The response for listing job run results.

value.description: The list of device result entries.

nextLink.description: The link to the next page of results.

JobRunResults.description: The results summary of a job run.

inProgress.description: Number of targets currently in progress.

canceled.description: Number of targets whose execution was canceled.

notApplied.description: Number of targets the operation does not apply to. Update jobs only; computed by ADR as total - (succeeded + failed + inProgress + canceled).

JobRunStatus.description: The status of a job run.

values.name: Queued
values.description: The job run is queued for dispatch and is consuming concurrency quota.

values.name: Active
values.description: The job run is actively executing.

values.name: TimedOut
values.description: The job run timed out before completing. Action/State jobs only; not applicable to Update jobs.

values.name: Canceled
values.description: The job run was canceled. See `cancellationReason` for the cause.

JobScheduleRequest.description: Request body for scheduling a job.

scheduledTime.description: The time at which the job should be scheduled to run.

timeout.description: The timeout duration for the job execution (ISO 8601 duration format).

JobTagsUpdate.description: The type used for updating tags in Job resources.

targetResourceId.description: The Azure resource ID of the target group. Must reference a Microsoft.DeviceRegistry/namespaces/groups resource.

values.name: Update
values.description: An update job. Targets a group of registered devices for ADU deployments.

values.name: RecoveryUpdate
values.description: A recovery update job. Targets all compatible devices for ADU deployments -- including devices not yet registered in ADR -- served exclusively via the ADU recovery device endpoint.

JobUpdateDefinition.description: The update definition for a job.

updateId.description: The Azure Device Update (ADU) update identity to deploy.

JobUpdateId.description: Identifier of an Azure Device Update (ADU) update.

name.description: The name of the update.

LeafCertificateConfigurationUpdate.description: Leaf certificate configuration for update operations.

name.description: Name of the action.

actionConfiguration.description: Stringified JSON that contains connector-specific configuration for the action.

targetUri.description: The target URI on which a client can invoke the specific action.

topic.description: The MQTT topic path on which a client will receive the request for the action.

defaultStreamsConfiguration.description: Stringified JSON that contains connector-specific default configuration for all streams. Each stream can have its own configuration that overrides the default settings here.

defaultManagementGroupsConfiguration.description: Stringified JSON that contains connector-specific default configuration for all management groups. Each management group can have its own configuration that overrides the default settings here.

datasets.description: Array of datasets that are part of the asset. Each dataset describes the data points that make up the set.

eventGroups.description: Array of event groups that are part of the asset. Each event group can have per-event group configuration.

streams.description: Array of streams that are part of the asset. Each stream can have a per-stream configuration.

managementGroups.description: Array of management groups that are part of the asset. Each management group can have a per-group configuration.

status.description: Read only object to reflect changes that have occurred on the Edge. Similar to Kubernetes status property for custom resources.

config.description: Defines the asset status config properties.

healthState.description: The details about the runtime health state of the asset.

name.description: The name of the dataset. Must be unique within the status.datasets array. This name is used to correlate between the spec and status dataset information.

error.description: Object to transfer and persist errors that originate from the edge.

requestMessageSchemaReference.description: The request message schema reference object for the action.

responseMessageSchemaReference.description: The response message schema reference object for the action.

error.description: Object to transfer and persist errors that originate from the edge.

actions.description: Array of action statuses that describe the status of each action.

name.description: The name of the stream. Must be unique within the status.streams array. This name is used to correlate between the spec and status event information.

eventGroupConfiguration.description: Stringified JSON that contains connector-specific configuration for the event group. For OPC UA, this could include configuration like, publishingInterval, samplingInterval, and queueSize.

defaultDestinations.description: Destinations for events. Default destinations when destinations is not defined at the event level.

events.description: Array of events that are part of the event group.

name.description: Name of the action.

actionConfiguration.description: Stringified JSON that contains connector-specific configuration for the action.

targetUri.description: The target URI on which a client can invoke the specific action.

topic.description: The MQTT topic path on which a client will receive the request for the action.

lastUpdatedOn.description: Timestamp (in UTC) indicating when the management action was added or modified.

name.description: Name of the management group.

managementGroupConfiguration.description: Stringified JSON that contains connector-specific configuration for the management group.

dataSource.description: Reference to a data source for a given management group.

defaultTopic.description: Default MQTT topic path on which a client will receive the request for all actions that are part of the management group.

defaultTimeoutInSeconds.description: Default response timeout for all actions that are part of the management group.

actions.description: Array of actions that are part of the management group. Each action can have an individual configuration.

lastUpdatedOn.description: Timestamp (in UTC) indicating when the management group was added or modified.

name.description: Name of the stream definition.

streamConfiguration.description: Stringified JSON that contains connector-specific configuration for the specific stream.

lastUpdatedOn.description: Timestamp (in UTC) indicating when the stream was added or modified.

name.description: The name of the event.

dataSource.description: Reference to a data source for a given event.

eventConfiguration.description: Stringified JSON that contains connector-specific configuration for the event. For OPC UA, this could include configuration like, publishingInterval, samplingInterval, and queueSize.

NamespaceEventDataPoint.description: Defines the event data point properties.

name.description: The name of the data point.

dataSource.description: The address of the source of the data in the asset (e.g. URL) so that a client can access the data source on the asset.

dataPointConfiguration.description: Stringified JSON that contains connector-specific configuration for the data point. For OPC UA, this could include configuration like, publishingInterval, samplingInterval, and queueSize.

name.description: The name of the event group.

dataSource.description: The address of the notifier of the event group in the asset (e.g. URL) so that a client can access the event group on the asset.

eventGroupConfiguration.description: Stringified JSON that contains connector-specific configuration for the event group. For OPC UA, this could include configuration like, publishingInterval, samplingInterval, and queueSize.

defaultDestinations.description: Destinations for events. Default destinations when destinations is not defined at the event level.

events.description: Array of events that are part of the event group.

values.name: Resources
values.description: Scoping the migration to resourceIds provided

version.description: A read-only incremental counter indicating the number of times the configuration has been modified from the perspective of the current actual (edge) state of the CRD. Edge would be the only writer of this value and would sync back up to the cloud. In steady state, this should equal version.

lastTransitionTime.description: A read-only timestamp indicating the last time the configuration has been modified from the perspective of the current actual (edge) state of the CRD. Edge would be the only writer of this value and would sync back up to the cloud.

error.description: Object to transfer and persist errors that originate from the edge.

StatusError.description: Defines the status config error properties.

code.description: Error code for classification of errors (ex: '400', '404', '500', etc.).

message.description: Human-readable helpful error message to provide additional context for error (e.g.,: “Capability ID 'foo' does not exist”).

details.description: Array of error details that describe the status of each error.

StorageDestinationConfiguration.description: The configuration for a storage destination.

StreamDestinationTarget.description: The set of supported stream destinations for an asset.

StreamMqttDestination.description: The type for a MQTT destination.

StreamStorageDestination.description: The type for a storage destination.

Topic.description: Object that describes the topic information.

path.description: The topic path for messages published to an MQTT broker.

retain.description: When set to 'Keep', messages published to an MQTT broker will have the retain flag set. Default: 'Never'.

TrustSettings.description: Defines server trust settings for an endpoint.

trustList.description: Defines a secret reference for certificates to trust.

UpdateJobDefinition.description: Update-job definition. Used when jobType is Update.

schedulingType.description: The scheduling type for the Update job. Update jobs are always continuous: ADU deployments target the group until superseded, canceled, or the parent job is deleted.

update.description: The update definition for the job.

UpdateJobProperties.description: Job properties for an Update job.

target.description: The target of the job. Set at create time; immutable thereafter.

definition.description: The update definition for the job. Set at create time; immutable thereafter.

UpdateJobSchedulingType.description: Scheduling types supported by an Update job. Only continuous is supported.

values.name: Continuous
values.description: The job runs continuously against the target group until superseded, canceled, or the parent job is deleted.
